{\n1139\t\t\tc.SecurityContext = securityContext\n1140\t\t}\n1141\t}\n1142\t\n1143\t\n1144\t// =============================================================================\n1145\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n1146\t// =============================================================================\n1147\t\n1148\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\n1149\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n1150\t\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n1151\t\tif err != nil {\n1152\t\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n1153\t\t}\n1154\t\n1155\t\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n1156\t\tif err != nil {\n1157\t\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n1158\t\t\tdesc = \"\"\n1159\t\t}\n1160\t\n1161\t\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n1162\t}\n1163\t\n1164\t// =============================================================================\n1165\t// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n1166\t// =============================================================================\n1167\t\n1168\t// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n1169\t// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\n1170\ttype errorTypeTracker struct {\n1171\t\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n1172\t\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n1173\t}\n1174\t\n1175\t// global tracker instance\n1176\tvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n1177\t\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n1178\t}\n1179\t\n1180\t// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n1181\t// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n1182\t// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n1183\t//\n1184\t// Parameters:\n1185\t//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n1186\t//\n1187\t// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\n1188\tfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n1189\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n1190\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n1191\t\n1192\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n1193\t\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n1194\t}\n1195\t\n1196\t// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n1197\t// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n1198\t// configuration issues.\n1199\t//\n1200\t// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\n1201\tfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n1202\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n1203\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n1204\t\n1205\t\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n1206\t\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n1207\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n1208\t\t}\n1209\t\treturn result\n1210\t}\n1211\t\n1212\t// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n1213\t// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\n1214\tfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n1215\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n1216\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n1217\t\n1218\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n1219\t}\n1220\t\n1221\t// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n1222\t// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\n1223\tfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n1224\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n1225\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n1226\t\n1227\t\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n1228\t}\n1229\t\n1230\t// =============================================================================\n1231\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n1232\t// =============================================================================\n1233\t\n1234\t// FormatError creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum.\n1235\t// This is the primary error formatting function that provides type-safe error classification.\n1236\t//\n1237\t// This function uses ErrorType enum for consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n1238\t// For backward compatibility with string-based error types, use FormatErrorString.\n1239\t//\n1240\t// ErrorType to Message Mapping:\n1241\t// The ErrorType enum provides type-safe error classification with the following mappings:\n1242\t//   - ErrTypeRequired (required): Required field is missing or empty\n1243\t//   - ErrTypeFormat (format): Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n1244\t//   - ErrTypeRange (range): Value is outside acceptable numeric range (min/max)\n1245\t//   - ErrTypeLength (length): String length or collection size is invalid\n1246\t//   - ErrTypeType (type): Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n1247\t//   - ErrTypeValue (value): Value is invalid for domain-specific reasons\n1248\t//   - ErrTypeDuplicate (duplicate): Duplicate value detected\n1249\t//   - ErrTypeConflict (conflict): Conflict with existing values or constraints\n1250\t//   - ErrTypeUnknown (unknown): Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n1251\t//\n1252\t// Parameters:\n1253\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n1254\t//   - message: The error message content\n1255\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n1256\t//\n1257\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure: \"[errorType] fieldName: message\"\n1258\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1259\t//\n1260\t// Example usage:\n1261\t//\n1262\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1263\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n1264\t//\n1265\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n1266\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n1267\t//\n1268\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n1269\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n1270\t//\n1271\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"\", \"email\")\n1272\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: email validation failed\" (empty message fallback)\n1273\tfunc FormatError(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1274\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n1275\t\t// This converts the typed ErrorType enum to its string representation\n1276\t\t// for consistent message formatting across the codebase\n1277\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n1278\t\n1279\t\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n1280\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1281\t\n1282\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1283\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1284\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n1285\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1286\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1287\t\t\t} else {\n1288\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1289\t\t\t}\n1290\t\t}\n1291\t\n1292\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n1293\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n1294\t}\n1295\t\n1296\t// FormatErrorString creates a formatted error message using string error type.\n1297\t// This function provides backward compatibility for code that uses string-based error types.\n1298\t//\n1299\t// This function validates string error types against the ErrorType enum for type-safe\n1300\t// error classification while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value.\n1301\t//\n1302\t// Error Type Validation:\n1303\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n1304\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n1305\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n1306\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n1307\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n1308\t//\n1309\t// Parameters:\n1310\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error as a string (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n1311\t//   - message: The error message content\n1312\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (variadic parameter)\n1313\t//\n1314\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n1315\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1316\t//\n1317\t// Example usage:\n1318\t//\n1319\t//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n1320\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1321\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n1322\t//\n1323\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\")\n1324\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n1325\t//\n1326\t//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n1327\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n1328\t//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n1329\t//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n1330\t//\n1331\t//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n1332\t//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n1333\t//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n1334\tfunc FormatErrorString(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n1335\t\t// Extract field name from variadic args first\n1336\t\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n1337\t\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n1338\t\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n1339\t\t}\n1340\t\n1341\t\t// Check if errorType is whitespace-only (not truly empty)\n1342\t\t// Whitespace-only error types should be tracked as invalid\n1343\t\t// because they are likely user errors, not intentional defaults\n1344\t\toriginalHasContent := errorType != \"\"\n1345\t\ttrimmedType := strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n1346\t\tisWhitespaceOnly := originalHasContent && trimmedType == \"\"\n1347\t\n1348\t\t// Track whitespace-only error types as invalid\n1349\t\tif isWhitespaceOnly {\n1350\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n1351\t\t}\n1352\t\n1353\t\t// Trim whitespace from errorType before validation\n1354\t\terrorType = trimmedType\n1355\t\n1356\t\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n1357\t\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n1358\t\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n1359\t\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n1360\t\n1361\t\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n1362\t\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the trimmed errorType wasn't \"unknown\" (case-insensitive),\n1363\t\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n1364\t\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && !strings.EqualFold(errorType, \"unknown\") {\n1365\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n1366\t\t}\n1367\t\n1368\t\t// Trim whitespace from message and fieldName before checking\n1369\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1370\t\tfieldNameStr = strings.TrimSpace(fieldNameStr)\n1371\t\n1372\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1373\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1374\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided (after trimming)\n1375\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n1376\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n1377\t\t\t} else {\n1378\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1379\t\t\t}\n1380\t\t}\n1381\t\n1382\t\t// Handle empty errorType (after trimming) - use fallback\n1383\t\tif errorType == \"\" {\n1384\t\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n1385\t\t}\n1386\t\n1387\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the processed error type string\n1388\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n1389\t}\n1390\t\n1391\t// FormatErrorWithType is an alias for FormatError for backward compatibility.\n1392\t// This function calls FormatError with the same parameters.\n1393\t//\n1394\t// Deprecated: Use FormatError directly with ErrorType enum parameter instead.\n1395\t// This function is maintained for backward compatibility with existing code.\n1396\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1397\t\treturn FormatError(errorType, message, fieldName)\n1398\t}\n1399\t\n1400\t// =============================================================================\n1401\t// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\n1402\t// =============================================================================\n1403\t\n1404\t// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\n1405\ttype QuoteStyle string\n1406\t\n1407\tconst (\n1408\t\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\n1409\t\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \"\"\n1410\t\t// SingleQuote applies single quotes around field names\n1411\t\tSingleQuote QuoteStyle = \"'\"\n1412\t\t// DoubleQuote applies double quotes around field names\n1413\t\tDoubleQuote QuoteStyle = \"\\\"\"\n1414\t\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\n1415\t\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \"`\"\n1416\t)\n1417\t\n1418\t// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\n1419\ttype FieldRefConfig struct {\n1420\t\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\n1421\t\tprefix     string\n1422\t}\n1423\t\n1424\t// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\n1425\ttype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\n1426\t\n1427\t// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\n1428\t// The quote style is applied to each field name component (not to array indices or prefixes).\n1429\t//\n1430\t// Example usage:\n1431\t//\n1432\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1433\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n1434\t//\n1435\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n1436\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n1437\tfunc WithQuoteStyle(style QuoteStyle) FieldRefOption {\n1438\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n1439\t\t\tc.quoteStyle = style\n1440\t\t}\n1441\t}\n1442\t\n1443\t// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\n1444\t// The prefix is added before the field path (e.g., \"response\", \"request\", \"data\").\n1445\t// The prefix is not affected by quote styles and is added as-is.\n1446\t//\n1447\t// If both the prefix parameter and WithPrefix option are provided, the option takes precedence.\n1448\t//\n1449\t// Example usage:\n1450\t//\n1451\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"response\"))\n1452\t//\t// Returns: response.user.email\n1453\t//\n1454\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"request\"))\n1455\t//\t// Returns: request.email\n1456\t//\n1457\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"data\"), WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1458\t//\t// Returns: data.\"users\"[0].\"email\"\n1459\tfunc WithPrefix(prefix string) FieldRefOption {\n1460\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n1461\t\t\tc.prefix = prefix\n1462\t\t}\n1463\t}\n1464\t\n1465\t// =============================================================================\n1466\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n1467\t// =============================================================================\n1468\t\n1469\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n1470\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n1471\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n1472\t//\n1473\t// Parameters:\n1474\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n1475\t//\n1476\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n1477\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n1478\t//\n1479\t// Example usage:\n1480\t//\n1481\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n1482\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n1483\t//\n1484\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n1485\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n1486\t//\n1487\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n1488\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n1489\t//\n1490\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n1491\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n1492\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n1493\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n1494\t\n1495\t\t// Handle empty path\n1496\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n1497\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1498\t\t}\n1499\t\n1500\t\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n1501\t\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n1502\t}\n1503\t\n1504\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n1505\t// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\n1506\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n1507\t\tif path == \"\" {\n1508\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1509\t\t}\n1510\t\n1511\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n1512\t\tvar result []string\n1513\t\n1514\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n1515\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n1516\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n1517\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1518\t\t\t}\n1519\t\n1520\t\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n1521\t\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n1522\t\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n1523\t\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n1524\t\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n1525\t\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n1526\t\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n1527\t\t\t\t} else {\n1528\t\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n1529\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1530\t\t\t\t}\n1531\t\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n1532\t\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n1533\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1534\t\t\t} else {\n1535\t\t\t\t// Regular field name\n1536\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1537\t\t\t}\n1538\t\t}\n1539\t\n1540\t\tif len(result) == 0 {\n1541\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1542\t\t}\n1543\t\n1544\t\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n1545\t}\n1546\t\n1547\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\n1548\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n1549\t\tif s == \"\" {\n1550\t\t\treturn false\n1551\t\t}\n1552\t\n1553\t\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n1554\t\tif s[0] == '-' {\n1555\t\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n1556\t\t\t\treturn false\n1557\t\t\t}\n1558\t\t\ts = s[1:]\n1559\t\t}\n1560\t\n1561\t\t// Check if all characters are digits\n1562\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n1563\t\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n1564\t\t\t\treturn false\n1565\t\t\t}\n1566\t\t}\n1567\t\n1568\t\treturn true\n1569\t}\n1570\t\n1571\t// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\n1572\tfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n1573\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n1574\t}\n1575\t\n1576\t// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an optional prefix.\n1577\t// This function formats field paths for consistent display in error messages, normalizing\n1578\t// array notation and handling edge cases gracefully. It supports optional quote styles\n1579\t// for field names through variadic options.\n1580\t//\n1581\t// Parameters:\n1582\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n1583\t//   - prefix: Optional field prefix to add (e.g., \"request\", \"response\", \"\" for no prefix)\n1584\t//   - options: Optional functions to configure formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1585\t//\n1586\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n1587\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" (or \"prefix.(unknown field)\" if prefix provided).\n1588\t//\n1589\t// Example usage:\n1590\t//\n1591\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\")\n1592\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n1593\t//\n1594\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\")\n1595\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n1596\t//\n1597\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"request\")\n1598\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n1599\t//\n1600\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n1601\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n1602\t//\n1603\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1604\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n1605\t//\n1606\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n1607\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n1608\t//\n1609\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"data.items\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(Backtick))\n1610\t//\t// Returns: `data`.`items`\n1611\t//\n1612\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"\")\n1613\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n1614\t//\n1615\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"request\")\n1616\t//\t// Returns: \"request\"\n1617\tfunc FormatFieldReference(fieldPath string, prefix string, options ...FieldRefOption) string {\n1618\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n1619\t\tprefix = strings.TrimSpace(prefix)\n1620\t\n1621\t\t// Apply options\n1622\t\tconfig := &FieldRefConfig{quoteStyle: NoQuote, prefix: \"\"}\n1623\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n1624\t\t\topt(config)\n1625\t\t}\n1626\t\n1627\t\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n1628\t\tif config.prefix != \"\" {\n1629\t\t\tprefix = config.prefix\n1630\t\t}\n1631\t\n1632\t\t// Handle empty field path\n1633\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n1634\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1635\t\t\t\t// Normalize prefix even when field is empty\n1636\t\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n1637\t\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix != \"(unknown field)\" {\n1638\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPrefix\n1639\t\t\t\t}\n1640\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n1641\t\t\t}\n1642\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1643\t\t}\n1644\t\n1645\t\t// Normalize field path to handle array indices\n1646\t\tnormalizedPath := FormatFieldPath(fieldPath)\n1647\t\n1648\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n1649\t\tif normalizedPath == \"(unknown field)\" {\n1650\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1651\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n1652\t\t\t}\n1653\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1654\t\t}\n1655\t\n1656\t\t// Apply quote style if specified\n1657\t\tif config.quoteStyle != NoQuote {\n1658\t\t\tnormalizedPath = applyQuoteStyle(normalizedPath, config.quoteStyle)\n1659\t\t}\n1660\t\n1661\t\t// Add prefix if provided\n1662\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1663\t\t\t// Normalize prefix as well in case it contains array indices\n1664\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n1665\t\n1666\t\t\t// If prefix normalizes to unknown field, just return the field path\n1667\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix == \"(unknown field)\" {\n1668\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1669\t\t\t}\n1670\t\n1671\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedPrefix, normalizedPath)\n1672\t\t}\n1673\t\n1674\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1675\t}\n1676\t\n1677\t// applyQuoteStyle applies quotes to field names in a normalized path.\n1678\t// Array indices are NOT quoted (e.g., \"users[0].email\" becomes \"'users'[0].'email'\").\n1679\tfunc applyQuoteStyle(path string, style QuoteStyle) string {\n1680\t\tif path == \"(unknown field)\" || path == \"\" {\n1681\t\t\treturn path\n1682\t\t}\n1683\t\n1684\t\tvar result strings.Builder\n1685\t\tvar fieldName strings.Builder\n1686\t\tinBrackets := false\n1687\t\n1688\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(path); i++ {\n1689\t\t\tr := rune(path[i])\n1690\t\n1691\t\t\tswitch {\n1692\t\t\tcase r == '.':\n1693\t\t\t\t// End of field name, add quoted version\n1694\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1695\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1696\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1697\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1698\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1699\t\t\t\t}\n1700\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('.')\n1701\t\n1702\t\t\tcase r == '[':\n1703\t\t\t\t// Start of array index, flush field name first\n1704\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1705\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1706\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1707\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1708\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1709\t\t\t\t}\n1710\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('[')\n1711\t\t\t\tinBrackets = true\n1712\t\n1713\t\t\tcase r == ']':\n1714\t\t\t\t// End of array index, don't quote\n1715\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(']')\n1716\t\t\t\tinBrackets = false\n1717\t\n1718\t\t\tdefault:\n1719\t\t\t\t// Only accumulate field name characters when NOT inside brackets\n1720\t\t\t\tif !inBrackets {\n1721\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.WriteRune(r)\n1722\t\t\t\t} else {\n1723\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(r)\n1724\t\t\t\t}\n1725\t\t\t}\n1726\t\t}\n1727\t\n1728\t\t// Don't forget the last field name\n1729\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1730\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1731\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1732\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1733\t\t}\n1734\t\n1735\t\treturn result.String()\n1736\t}\n1737\t\n1738\t// =============================================================================\n1739\t// CATEGORY-AWARE FORMATTING WITH SEVERITY\n1740\t// =============================================================================\n1741\t\n1742\t// FormatErrorWithSeverity creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum\n1743\t// with explicit severity support. This function provides type-safe error classification\n1744\t// with severity-based formatting.\n1745\t//\n1746\t// This function respects error severity levels and applies appropriate formatting:\n1747\t// - Critical errors: Bold styling with prominent indicators\n1748\t// - High errors: Warning indicators with emphasis\n1749\t// - Medium errors: Standard formatting with indicators\n1750\t// - Low errors: Subtle formatting\n1751\t// - Info errors: Minimal formatting\n1752\t//\n1753\t// Parameters:\n1754\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n1755\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity level (e.g., SeverityCritical, SeverityHigh)\n1756\t//   - message: The error message content\n1757\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n1758\t//\n1759\t// Returns a formatted error message string with severity-based styling.\n1760\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1761\t//\n1762\t// Example usage:\n1763\t//\n1764\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithSeverity(validate.ErrTypeRequired, validate.SeverityHigh, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1765\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] HIGH [required] email: This field is required\"\n1766\t//\n1767\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithSeverity(validate.ErrTypeFormat, validate.SeverityMedium, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n1768\t//\t// Returns: \"[■] MED [format] Invalid email format\"\n1769\tfunc FormatErrorWithSeverity(errorType ErrorType, severity ErrorSeverity, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1770\t\t// Get default severity if not provided\n1771\t\tif severity == \"\" {\n1772\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1773\t\t}\n1774\t\n1775\t\t// Trim whitespace from inputs\n1776\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n1777\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1778\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n1779\t\n1780\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1781\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1782\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1783\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1784\t\t\t} else {\n1785\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1786\t\t\t}\n1787\t\t}\n1788\t\n1789\t\t// Build severity prefix\n1790\t\tseverityPrefix := formatSeverityPrefix(severity)\n1791\t\n1792\t\t// Build the formatted message\n1793\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n1794\t\n1795\t\t// Add severity indicator and tag\n1796\t\tbuilder.WriteString(severityPrefix)\n1797\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1798\t\n1799\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n1800\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorTypeStr))\n1801\t\n1802\t\t// Add field name if present\n1803\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1804\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n1805\t\t}\n1806\t\n1807\t\t// Add message\n1808\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1809\t\n1810\t\treturn builder.String()\n1811\t}\n1812\t\n1813\t// formatSeverityPrefix creates a severity prefix with indicator and tag.\n1814\tfunc formatSeverityPrefix(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1815\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n1816\t\ttag := severityTag(severity)\n1817\t\n1818\t\tif indicator != \"\" && tag != \"\" {\n1819\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s %s]\", indicator, tag)\n1820\t\t} else if indicator != \"\" {\n1821\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", indicator)\n1822\t\t} else if tag != \"\" {\n1823\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", tag)\n1824\t\t}\n1825\t\n1826\t\treturn \"\"\n1827\t}\n1828\t\n1829\t// severityTag returns a short tag prefix for severity levels.\n1830\tfunc severityTag(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1831\t\tswitch severity {\n1832\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n1833\t\t\treturn \"CRIT\"\n1834\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n1835\t\t\treturn \"HIGH\"\n1836\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n1837\t\t\treturn \"MED\"\n1838\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n1839\t\t\treturn \"LOW\"\n1840\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n1841\t\t\treturn \"INFO\"\n1842\t\tdefault:\n1843\t\t\treturn \"UNK\"\n1844\t\t}\n1845\t}\n1846\t\n1847\t// FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity creates a formatted error message with\n1848\t// both category and severity awareness. This is the most comprehensive formatting\n1849\t// function that respects both error categorization and severity levels.\n1850\t//\n1851\t// This function applies category-specific formatting:\n1852\t// - HTTP errors: Technical focus with status code details\n1853\t// - Content errors: Response structure and body focus\n1854\t// - Validation errors: Field-level validation focus\n1855\t// - Performance errors: Timing and rate limit focus\n1856\t// - Security errors: Authentication and authorization focus\n1857\t//\n1858\t// Combined with severity-based styling for maximum clarity.\n1859\t//\n1860\t// Parameters:\n1861\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n1862\t//   - message: The error message content\n1863\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n1864\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions for customization\n1865\t//\n1866\t// Returns a formatted error message with category and severity awareness.\n1867\t//\n1868\t// Example usage:\n1869\t//\n1870\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1871\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n1872\t//\t    \"Field is required\",\n1873\t//\t    \"email\",\n1874\t//\t    validate.WithSeverityOverride(validate.SeverityCritical),\n1875\t//\t    validate.WithCategoryHint(validate.CategoryValidation),\n1876\t//\t)\n1877\t//\t// Returns: \"[🚨 CRIT] [Validation] [required] email: Field is required\"\n1878\tfunc FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1879\t\terrorType ErrorType,\n1880\t\tmessage string,\n1881\t\tfieldName string,\n1882\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n1883\t) string {\n1884\t\t// Get default category and severity\n1885\t\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1886\t\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1887\t\n1888\t\t// Apply options to override defaults\n1889\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n1890\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n1891\t\t\topt(config)\n1892\t\t}\n1893\t\n1894\t\t// Apply overrides if specified\n1895\t\tif config.SeverityOverride != \"\" {\n1896\t\t\tseverity = config.SeverityOverride\n1897\t\t}\n1898\t\tif config.CategoryHint != \"\" {\n1899\t\t\tcategory = config.CategoryHint\n1900\t\t}\n1901\t\n1902\t\t// Trim whitespace from inputs\n1903\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1904\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n1905\t\n1906\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1907\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1908\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1909\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1910\t\t\t} else {\n1911\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1912\t\t\t}\n1913\t\t}\n1914\t\n1915\t\t// Build severity prefix\n1916\t\tseverityPrefix := formatSeverityPrefix(severity)\n1917\t\n1918\t\t// Build category-specific formatting\n1919\t\tcategoryPrefix := formatCategoryPrefix(category)\n1920\t\n1921\t\t// Build the formatted message\n1922\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n1923\t\n1924\t\t// Add severity indicator and tag\n1925\t\tbuilder.WriteString(severityPrefix)\n1926\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1927\t\n1928\t\t// Add category prefix if category is not custom\n1929\t\tif category != CategoryCustom && category != \"\" {\n1930\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(categoryPrefix)\n1931\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1932\t\t}\n1933\t\n1934\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n1935\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType.String()))\n1936\t\n1937\t\t// Add field name if present\n1938\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1939\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n1940\t\t}\n1941\t\n1942\t\t// Add message\n1943\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1944\t\n1945\t\t// Add category-specific suffix if applicable\n1946\t\tbuilder.WriteString(formatCategorySuffix(category, config))\n1947\t\n1948\t\treturn builder.String()\n1949\t}\n1950\t\n1951\t// formatCategoryPrefix creates a category-specific prefix.\n1952\tfunc formatCategoryPrefix(category ErrorCategory) string {\n1953\t\tswitch category {\n1954\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n1955\t\t\treturn \"[HTTP]\"\n1956\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n1957\t\t\treturn \"[Content]\"\n1958\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n1959\t\t\treturn \"[Validation]\"\n1960\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n1961\t\t\treturn \"[Performance]\"\n1962\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n1963\t\t\treturn \"[Security]\"\n1964\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n1965\t\t\treturn \"[Custom]\"\n1966\t\tdefault:\n1967\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1968\t\t}\n1969\t}\n1970\t\n1971\t// formatCategorySuffix creates a category-specific suffix with additional context.\n1972\tfunc formatCategorySuffix(category ErrorCategory, config *FormatConfig) string {\n1973\t\t// Handle nil config gracefully\n1974\t\tif config == nil {\n1975\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1976\t\t}\n1977\t\n1978\t\tvar suffix strings.Builder\n1979\t\n1980\t\tswitch category {\n1981\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n1982\t\t\t// Add HTTP-specific context\n1983\t\t\tif config.StatusCode != 0 {\n1984\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (status: %d)\", config.StatusCode))\n1985\t\t\t}\n1986\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n1987\t\t\t// Add performance-specific context\n1988\t\t\tif config.Timeout > 0 {\n1989\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (timeout: %dms)\", config.Timeout))\n1990\t\t\t}\n1991\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n1992\t\t\t// Add security-specific context\n1993\t\t\tif config.SecurityContext != \"\" {\n1994\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (security: %s)\", config.SecurityContext))\n1995\t\t\t}\n1996\t\t}\n1997\t\n1998\t\treturn suffix.String()\n1999\t}\n2000\t\n2001\t// =============================================================================\n2002\t// STREAMLINED VALIDATION ERROR FORMATTING HELPER\n2003\t// =============================================================================\n2004\t\n2005\t// FormatValidationErrorHelper is a streamlined helper function for formatting validation errors consistently.\n2006\t// This function provides a simple, unified interface for creating validation errors with optional\n2007\t// context, expected/actual values, and custom suggestions.\n2008\t//\n2009\t// This helper is designed to be the primary entry point for validation error formatting,\n2010\t// offering a balance between simplicity and flexibility. It handles the most common validation\n2011\t// error scenarios with a clean, easy-to-use API.\n2012\t//\n2013\t// # Parameters\n2014\t//\n2015\t//   - errorType: The category of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n2016\t//     Required. This identifies what kind of validation was performed.\n2017\t//\n2018\t//   - expected: The expected value (can be any type: int, string, []int, etc.)\n2019\t//     Optional. Pass nil if not applicable for your validation type.\n2020\t//\n2021\t//   - actual: The actual value received (can be any type)\n2022\t//     Optional. Pass nil if not applicable for your validation type.\n2023\t//\n2024\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions for customization (variadic parameter)\n2025\t//     Use validation helper options like WithContext(), WithSuggestions(), etc.\n2026\t//\n2027\t// # Returns\n2028\t//\n2029\t// A ValidationError struct populated with all provided fields that implements\n2030\t// the error interface and can be used immediately in error handling, logging, or test assertions.\n2031\t//\n2032\t// # Features\n2033\t//\n2034\t//   - **Unified Interface**: Single function for all validation error types\n2035\t//   - **Type Safety**: Works with any value type for expected/actual\n2036\t//   - **Auto-generated Suggestions**: Smart suggestions based on error type and values\n2037\t//   - **Custom Suggestions**: Override with domain-specific suggestions\n2038\t//   - **Flexible Context**: Add contextual information for debugging\n2039\t//   - **Optional Fields**: Only provide what you need for your scenario\n2040\t//\n2041\t// # Common Usage Patterns\n2042\t//\n2043\t// Basic usage with just error type and values:\n2044\t//\n2045\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"status_code\", 200, 404)\n2046\t//\n2047\t// With context for debugging:\n2048\t//\n2049\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"status_code\", 200, 404,\n2050\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\"))\n2051\t//\n2052\t// With custom suggestions:\n2053\t//\n2054\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"custom_field\", can:\n2096\t//\n2097\t//\t// Return directly as error\n2098\t//\tif err != nil {\n2099\t//\t    return err\n2100\t//\t}\n2101\t//\n2102\t//\t// Use in logging\n2103\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\n2104\t//\n2105\t//\t// Use in test assertions\n2106\t//\tassert.Equal(t, \"expected message\", err.Error())\n2107\t//\n2108\t// # Auto-generated Suggestions\n2109\t//\n2110\t// If suggestions\n2113\t//   - \"error_message\" with pattern mismatch: Pattern debugging suggestions\n2114\t//   - \"content_type\": Header and format verification suggestions\n2115\t//   - \"status_code_range\": Range-specific debugging suggestions\n2116\t//   - Custom types: Generic validation troubleshooting suggestions\n2117\t//\n2118\t// # When to Use This Helper\n2119\t//\n2120\t// Use FormatValidationErrorHelper when you need:\n2121\t//   - A simple, clean API for validation errors\n2122\t//   - Consistent error formatting across your codebase\n2123\t//   - Type-safe error creation with any value types\n2124\t//   - Optional context and suggestions without complexity\n2125\t//   - A unified entry point for all validation error scenarios\n2126\t//\n2127\t// For advanced scenarios requiring full control over all fields, use\n2128\t// NewValidationFormatter and the builder pattern instead.\n2129\tfunc FormatValidationErrorHelper(errorType string, expected, actual validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"sync\"\n)\n\n// ValidationFormatter provides a simplified, fluent API for formatting validation errors consistently.\n//\n// This builder wraps the ValidationError functionality with an ergonomic interface that allows\n// you to construct validation errors step-by-step using method chaining.\n//\n// # Design Pattern\n//\n// ValidationFormatter uses the builder pattern to provide:\n//   - Fluent, chainable methods for readable code\n//   - Auto-generated suggestions when not manually specified\n//   - Type-safe error construction\n//   - Consistent error format across all validation types\n//\n// # Required Fields\n//\n// Only validationType is required (via NewValidationFormatter). All other fields are optional.\n// If suggestions are not provided via WithSuggestions, they will be auto-generated based on\n// the validation type and expected/actual values.\n//\n// # Common Usage Pattern\n//\n// The typical workflow is:\n//  1. Create formatter with NewValidationFormatter(type)\n//  2. Chain With* methods to set optional fields\n//  3. Call Format() to generate the ValidationError\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Basic usage with auto-generated suggestions\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n//\t    WithExpected(200).\n//\t    WithActual(404).\n//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n//\t    Format()\n//\n//\t// With custom suggestions\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_field\").\n//\t    WithExpected(\"required\").\n//\t    WithActual(\"\").\n//\t    WithFieldName(\"email\").\n//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Email is required\", \"Check user input\").\n//\t    Format()\n//\n// # Extending for New Validation Types\n//\n// To add support for a new validation type:\n//  1. Use NewValidationFormatter(\"your_type\") with a descriptive type name\n//  2. Set appropriate fields using With* methods\n//  3. Optionally provide custom suggestions via WithSuggestions\n//  4. The formatter will automatically include the type in error output\n//\n// # Fields\n//\n// All fields are private to ensure the builder pattern is used correctly.\n// Access them through the public With* methods and Format().\ntype ValidationFormatter struct {\n\tvalidationType string\n\texpected       interface{}\n\tactual         interface{}\n\tcontext        string\n\tresponseSnippet string\n\tfieldName      string\n\tpatternDetails string\n\trangeInfo      string\n\tvalidationDetails []string\n\tcustomSuggestions []string\n}\n\n// NewValidationFormatter creates a new ValidationFormatter for the given validation type.\n//\n// This is the entry point for the builder pattern. The validationType identifies what\n// kind of validation was performed and is used to:\n//   - Categorize the error in the error message\n//   - Generate appropriate default suggestions\n//   - Provide context for error handling\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - validationType: The category of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n//\n//     Common validation types include:\n//     - \"status_code\": HTTP status code validation\n//     - \"error_message\": Error message pattern matching\n//     - \"content_type\": Content-Type header validation\n//     - \"status_code_range\": Status code range validation (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n//     - \"cors_headers\": CORS header validation\n//     - \"response_structure\": Response body structure validation\n//     - Custom types for domain-specific validation\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// A pointer to a new ValidationFormatter instance ready for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example Usage\n//\n//\t// Basic status code validation\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n//\t    WithExpected(200).\n//\t    WithActual(404).\n//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n//\t    Format()\n//\n//\t// Content-Type validation with context\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"content_type\").\n//\t    WithExpected(\"application/json\").\n//\t    WithActual(\"text/html\").\n//\t    WithContext(\"API response\").\n//\t    Format()\n//\n//\t// Custom validation type with all options\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_field_check\").\n//\t    WithExpected(\"non_empty\").\n//\t    WithActual(\"\").\n//\t    WithFieldName(\"username\").\n//\t    WithContext(\"User creation\").\n//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Username cannot be empty\", \"Must be 3-20 characters\").\n//\t    Format()\nfunc NewValidationFormatter(validationType string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\treturn &ValidationFormatter{\n\t\tvalidationType: validationType,\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithExpected sets the expected value for validation.\n//\n// This method specifies what value was expected during the validation check.\n// The expected value is compared against the actual value to provide clear\n// error messages showing what went wrong.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - expected: The expected value (can be any type: int, string, []int, etc.)\n//\n//     Common types:\n//     - int: Single expected value (e.g., expected status code 200)\n//     - []int: Multiple acceptable values (e.g., []int{200, 201, 204})\n//     - string: Expected pattern or literal value (e.g., \"application/json\")\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Single expected value\n//\tformatter.WithExpected(200)\n//\n//\t// Multiple acceptable values\n//\tformatter.WithExpected([]int{200, 201, 204})\n//\n//\t// String pattern\n//\tformatter.WithExpected(\"application/json\")\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithExpected(expected interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.expected = expected\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithActual sets the actual value received during validation.\n//\n// This method specifies what value was actually received during the validation check.\n// The actual value is compared against the expected value to show the discrepancy\n// that caused the validation failure.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - actual: The actual value received (can be any type: int, string, etc.)\n//\n//     Common types:\n//     - int: Actual numeric value (e.g., actual status code 404)\n//     - string: Actual message or content received\n//     - Other types as appropriate for the validation context\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Status code that was received\n//\tformatter.WithActual(404)\n//\n//\t// Error message that was received\n//\tformatter.WithActual(\"access denied\")\n//\n//\t// Content-Type that was received\n//\tformatter.WithActual(\"text/html\")\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithActual(actual interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.actual = actual\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithContext sets additional context about the validation operation.\n//\n// Context provides valuable information about where or when the validation occurred,\n// making errors easier to debug and understand. This is especially useful in logs\n// and test output where multiple validations might be happening.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - context: A descriptive string explaining the validation context\n//\n//     Common context examples:\n//     - HTTP endpoints: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n//     - Operation descriptions: \"OAuth token validation\", \"User creation\"\n//     - Test scenarios: \"Positive case test\", \"Negative case test\"\n//     - Component names: \"Authentication service\", \"Database query\"\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// HTTP endpoint context\n//\tformatter.WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\")\n//\n//\t// Operation context\n//\tformatter.WithContext(\"OAuth token validation\")\n//\n//\t// Test scenario context\n//\tformatter.WithContext(\"Testing unauthorized access\")\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithContext(context string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.context = context\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithResponseSnippet sets a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n//\n// Response snippets provide crucial debugging information by showing the actual\n// content that caused the validation failure. This is particularly useful when\n// debugging complex response structures or unexpected error messages.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - snippet: A truncated excerpt from the response (recommended: 100-200 characters)\n//\n//     Best practices:\n//     - Keep snippets concise (100-200 characters recommended)\n//     - Include relevant parts that show the problem\n//     - Truncate long responses with \"...\" to keep errors readable\n//     - Sanitize sensitive data before including in snippets\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Short JSON response snippet\n//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"error\": \"invalid_token\", \"code\": \"AUTH_001\"}`)\n//\n//\t// Truncated HTML snippet\n//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(`<html><body>Error: Page not found...</body></html>`)\n//\n//\t// Error message snippet\n//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(\"Access denied: insufficient permissions for resource\")\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.responseSnippet = snippet\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithFieldName sets the specific field name where the error was found.\n//\n// This method identifies the exact field that failed validation, which is especially\n// useful when validating structured data like JSON responses, form fields, or\n// configuration objects.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - fieldName: The name of the field where the error occurred\n//\n//     Common examples:\n//     - Response body fields: \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"code\"\n//     - Form fields: \"email\", \"password\", \"username\"\n//     - Nested fields: \"user.email\", \"data.items[0].id\"\n//     - Headers: \"Content-Type\", \"Authorization\"\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Simple field name\n//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"error\")\n//\n//\t// Nested field notation\n//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"user.email\")\n//\n//\t// Array field notation\n//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"items[0].id\")\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithFieldName(fieldName string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.fieldName = fieldName\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithPatternDetails sets information about pattern matching failures.\n//\n// This method provides additional context when validation involves pattern matching,\n// such as regex validation for error messages, format checking, or content verification.\n// It helps explain what pattern was expected and how the actual value didn't match.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - details: Information about the pattern that failed to match\n//\n//     Common examples:\n//     - Regex patterns: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n//     - Format requirements: \"expected format 'user@domain.com'\"\n//     - Pattern descriptions: \"must contain at least one uppercase letter\"\n//     - Match details: \"pattern matched at position 5 but failed validation\"\n//\n// # When to Use\n//\n// Use this method when:\n//   - Validating error messages against regex patterns\n//   - Checking string formats (email, phone, UUID, etc.)\n//   - Verifying content patterns in responses\n//   - Any validation involving pattern matching\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Regex pattern explanation\n//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\")\n//\n//\t// Format requirement\n//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"expected format: YYYY-MM-DD\")\n//\n//\t// Content pattern description\n//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"must contain at least 8 characters\")\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithPatternDetails(details string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.patternDetails = details\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithRangeInfo sets range boundaries for range validation failures.\n//\n// This method provides context when validating against ranges, such as HTTP status\n// code ranges (4xx, 5xx), numeric ranges, or value intervals. It helps explain the\n// expected boundaries and what range the actual value fell outside of.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - info: Description of the range boundaries\n//\n//     Common examples:\n//     - Status code ranges: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n//     - Numeric ranges: \"0-100\", \"1-1000\"\n//     - Descriptive ranges: \"positive numbers only\", \"non-negative integers\"\n//\n// # When to Use\n//\n// Use this method when:\n//   - Validating HTTP status code ranges (e.g., expecting 4xx, got 2xx)\n//   - Checking numeric value ranges (e.g., age 0-120)\n//   - Verifying values within intervals (e.g., percentage 0-100)\n//   - Any validation involving range constraints\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Status code range\n//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"400-499 (Client Error)\")\n//\n//\t// Numeric range\n//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"0-120\")\n//\n//\t// Descriptive range\n//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"positive integers only\")\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithRangeInfo(info string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.rangeInfo = info\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithValidationDetails adds additional validation-specific details.\n//\n// This method allows you to provide granular, multi-line information about what was\n// checked during validation and specifically what failed. This is useful for complex\n// validations that check multiple conditions or have detailed requirements.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - details: Variable number of detail strings to add to the error\n//\n//     Common uses:\n//     - List specific conditions that failed\n//     - Explain validation rules in detail\n//     - Provide step-by-step validation results\n//     - Document intermediate validation states\n//\n// # When to Use\n//\n// Use this method when:\n//   - Validation involves multiple checks or conditions\n//   - You need to explain complex validation rules\n//   - Detailed debugging information would be helpful\n//   - The validation logic is non-trivial\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Multiple specific failures\n//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n//\t    \"Password must be at least 8 characters\",\n//\t    \"Password must contain uppercase letters\",\n//\t    \"Password must contain special characters\",\n//\t)\n//\n//\t// Detailed explanation\n//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n//\t    \"Expected 200-299 status range\",\n//\t    \"Got 500 indicating server error\",\n//\t    \"Server logs show database connection failure\",\n//\t)\n//\n//\t// Step-by-step validation\n//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n//\t    \"Step 1: Check format - PASSED\",\n//\t    \"Step 2: Check length - FAILED\",\n//\t    \"Step 3: Check content - SKIPPED\",\n//\t)\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithValidationDetails(details ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.validationDetails = append(vf.validationDetails, details...)\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithSuggestions sets custom suggestions for fixing the validation failure.\n//\n// This method allows you to provide domain-specific, actionable recommendations for\n// resolving the validation error. Custom suggestions override the auto-generated\n// suggestions that would normally be created based on the validation type and values.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - suggestions: Variable number of suggestion strings\n//\n//     Best practices for suggestions:\n//     - Be specific and actionable (not just \"check the value\")\n//     - Provide concrete steps to fix the issue\n//     - Include debugging tips when relevant\n//     - Reference documentation or resources when applicable\n//     - Keep suggestions concise but informative\n//     - Order from most to least likely solution\n//\n// # When to Use Custom Suggestions\n//\n// Use this method when:\n//   - Domain-specific knowledge is needed (not generic suggestions)\n//   - You want to guide users to specific solutions\n//   - Auto-generated suggestions would be too generic\n//   - You need to reference internal documentation or systems\n//   - The fix requires specific domain expertise\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Domain-specific suggestions\n//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n//\t    \"Check if the OAuth token has expired\",\n//\t    \"Verify the token has 'read:users' scope\",\n//\t    \"Contact admin to refresh permissions\",\n//\t)\n//\n//\t// Specific debugging steps\n//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n//\t    \"Check service logs at /var/log/api/errors.log\",\n//\t    \"Verify database connection in config.yaml\",\n//\t    \"Test with curl: curl -v http://localhost:8080/health\",\n//\t)\n//\n//\t// Reference documentation\n//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n//\t    \"See API docs: https://docs.example.com/errors#404\",\n//\t    \"Check troubleshooting guide: /docs/troubleshooting.md\",\n//\t)\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.customSuggestions = append(vf.customSuggestions, suggestions...)\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// Format creates the final ValidationError with all configured options.\n//\n// This is the final method in the builder chain that constructs the ValidationError\n// with all the options that have been set. The returned ValidationError implements\n// the error interface and can be returned directly or used in error handling.\n//\n// # Behavior\n//\n// The Format() method:\n//   - Uses custom suggestions if provided via WithSuggestions\n//   - Auto-generates suggestions if no custom ones were set\n//   - Includes all fields that were set via With* methods\n//   - Returns a ValidationError ready for use\n//   - Implements error interface for immediate use\n//\n// # Auto-generated Suggestions\n//\n// If WithSuggestions was not called, suggestions are auto-generated based on:\n//   - validationType (e.g., status_code gets endpoint-checking suggestions)\n//   - expected and actual values (e.g., 404 gets resource-not-found suggestions)\n//   - Common patterns for the validation category\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// A ValidationError struct populated with all configured fields that implements\n// the error interface and can be used immediately in error handling, logging, or\n// test assertions.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Complete usage example\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n//\t    WithExpected(200).\n//\t    WithActual(404).\n//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n//\t    Format()\n//\n//\t// Error() method will produce:\n//\t// status_code validation failed\n//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n//\t//   Suggestions:\n//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n//\n//\t// Can be used directly as an error\n//\treturn err\n//\n//\t// Or formatted for logging\n//\tlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) Format() ValidationError {\n\tsuggestions := vf.customSuggestions\n\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n\t\t// Use enhanced auto-generated suggestions if no custom ones provided\n\t\tsuggestions = GenerateComprehensiveSuggestions(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual, vf.context)\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate a concise message from validation parts\n\tmessage := generateMessageFromParts(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\n\n\t// Construct ValidationError directly to support custom suggestions\n\treturn ValidationError{\n\t\tErrorType:         vf.validationType,\n\t\tMessage:           message,\n\t\tExpected:          vf.expected,\n\t\tActual:            vf.actual,\n\t\tContext:           vf.context,\n\t\tResponseSnippet:   vf.responseSnippet,\n\t\tFieldName:         vf.fieldName,\n\t\tPatternDetails:    vf.patternDetails,\n\t\tRangeInfo:         vf.rangeInfo,\n\t\tValidationDetails: vf.validationDetails,\n\t\tSuggestions:       suggestions,\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// EXTENDING THE FORMATTER FOR NEW VALIDATION TYPES\n// =============================================================================\n//\n// This section explains how to extend the ValidationFormatter to support new\n// validation types beyond the built-in convenience functions.\n//\n// # Creating Custom Validation Types\n//\n// To add support for a new validation type, you have several options:\n//\n// ## Option 1: Use the Builder Pattern Directly\n//\n// The simplest approach is to use NewValidationFormatter with your custom type:\n//\n//\t// Custom header validation\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_header\").\n//\t    WithExpected(\"X-Custom-Header: required\").\n//\t    WithActual(\"missing\").\n//\t    WithContext(\"API request validation\").\n//\t    WithFieldName(\"X-Custom-Header\").\n//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Add X-Custom-Header to requests\", \"See API docs\").\n//\t    Format()\n//\n// ## Option 2: Create a Convenience Function\n//\n// For commonly used validation types, create a convenience function following\n// the pattern of existing functions like FormatStatusCodeError:\n//\n//\t// FormatCustomHeaderError validates custom HTTP headers\n//\tfunc FormatCustomHeaderError(headerName, expected, actual, context string) validate.ValidationError {\n//\t    return validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_header\").\n//\t        WithExpected(expected).\n//\t        WithActual(actual).\n//\t        WithFieldName(headerName).\n//\t        WithContext(context).\n//\t        WithSuggestions(\n//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Add %s header to request\", headerName),\n//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected value: %s\", expected),\n//\t        ).\n//\t        Format()\n//\t}\n//\n// ## Option 3: Use FormatCustomValidationError\n//\n// For advanced scenarios requiring full control, use FormatCustomValidationError:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n//\t    \"response_time\",\n//\t    \"< 100ms\",\n//\t    \"250ms\",\n//\t    validate.WithContext(\"API performance check\"),\n//\t    validate.WithValidationDetails(\"SLA requires < 100ms response time\"),\n//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Optimize database queries\", \"Add caching\"),\n//\t)\n//\n// # Best Practices for New Validation Types\n//\n// When creating custom validation types:\n//\n// 1. **Use descriptive type names**: Choose clear, descriptive validation type names\n//    that indicate what was validated (e.g., \"response_time\", \"header_presence\").\n//\n// 2. **Provide specific suggestions**: Custom suggestions should be actionable and\n//    specific to your domain rather than generic.\n//\n// 3. **Include relevant context**: Always provide context about where/when the\n//    validation occurred to aid debugging.\n//\n// 4. **Use appropriate field types**: Match the expected/actual field types to what\n//    you're validating (int for codes, string for patterns, etc.).\n//\n// 5. **Consider validation details**: Use WithValidationDetails for complex\n//    validations with multiple steps or conditions.\n//\n// 6. **Document your validation type**: If creating a convenience function, document\n//    it thoroughly like the existing convenience functions.\n//\n// # Integration with Auto-generated Suggestions\n//\n// The ValidationFormatter automatically generates suggestions based on validation\n// type when you don't provide custom suggestions. To integrate your custom type\n// with this system, you can modify the generateSuggestions function to recognize\n// your new type and provide domain-appropriate suggestions.\n//\n// Current auto-generated suggestion patterns:\n//   - \"status_code\": Endpoint and resource verification suggestions\n//   - \"error_message\": Error analysis and debugging suggestions\n//   - \"content_type\": Header and format verification suggestions\n//   - \"status_code_range\": Range-specific debugging suggestions\n//\n// # Example: Adding a New Validation Type\n//\n// Here's a complete example of adding a \"response_time\" validation type:\n//\n//\t// 1. Create the convenience function\n//\tfunc FormatResponseTimeError(expectedMaxMs, actualMs int, endpoint string) validate.ValidationError {\n//\t    return validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"response_time\").\n//\t        WithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"< %dms\", expectedMaxMs)).\n//\t        WithActual(fmt.Sprintf(\"%dms\", actualMs)).\n//\t        WithContext(endpoint).\n//\t        WithValidationDetails(\n//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"SLA requirement: < %dms\", expectedMaxMs),\n//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Actual response time: %dms\", actualMs),\n//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"SLA violation: %dms over limit\", actualMs-expectedMaxMs),\n//\t        ).\n//\t        WithSuggestions(\n//\t            \"Check for slow database queries\",\n//\t            \"Verify network latency\",\n//\t            \"Review application performance metrics\",\n//\t            \"Consider implementing caching\",\n//\t        ).\n//\t        Format()\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// 2. Use it in your code\n//\terr := FormatResponseTimeError(100, 250, \"GET /api/users\")\n//\tlog.Printf(\"Performance validation failed: %v\", err)\n//\n// This approach ensures your custom validation types integrate seamlessly with\n// the existing error formatting system while providing domain-specific guidance.\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR COMMON VALIDATION SCENARIOS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - expected: The expected status code (e.g., 200) or codes (e.g., []int{200, 201})\n//   - actual: The actual status code received\n//   - context: Optional context about the request (e.g., \"GET /api/users\")\n//\n// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n//\t// Output:\n//\t// status_code validation failed\n//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n//\t//   Suggestions:\n//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\nfunc FormatStatusCodeError(expected interface{}, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n\t\tWithActual(actual).\n\t\tWithContext(context).\n\t\tFormat()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorMessageError creates a validation error for error message pattern mismatches.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - expectedPattern: The pattern that was expected (e.g., \"invalid.*token\", \"not found\")\n//   - actualMessage: The actual error message received\n//   - fieldName: The field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\")\n//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n//\n// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatErrorMessageError(\"invalid.*token\", \"access_denied\", \"error\", \"OAuth validation\")\n//\t// Output:\n//\t// error_message validation failed\n//\t//   Expected: invalid.*token\n//\t//   Actual:   access_denied\n//\t//   Context:  OAuth validation\n//\t//   Field:    error\n//\t//   Suggestions:\n//\t//     - Review the error message for specific details\n//\t//     - Check API documentation for this error type\n//\t//     - Verify request parameters match requirements\nfunc FormatErrorMessageError(expectedPattern, actualMessage, fieldName, context string) ValidationError {\n\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n\t\tWithExpected(expectedPattern).\n\t\tWithActual(actualMessage).\n\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n\t\tWithContext(context).\n\t\tFormat()\n}\n\n// FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n//   - actual: The actual status code received\n//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name being validated (e.g., \"status_code\", \"response_code\")\n//\n// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and suggestions.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(\"4xx\", 200, \"error response check\", \"status_code\")\n//\t// Output:\n//\t// status_code_range validation failed\n//\t//   Field:    status_code\n//\t//   Expected: 4xx (400-499)\n//\t//   Actual:   200\n//\t//   Context:  error response check\n//\t//   Suggestions:\n//\t//     - Review request parameters for errors\n//\t//     - Check authentication credentials\n//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\nfunc FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern string, actual int, context string, fieldName string) ValidationError {\n\tmin, max, desc, err := getRangeInfo(pattern)\n\n\t// Build range info string\n\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", min, max)\n\tif desc != \"\" {\n\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", min, max, desc)\n\t}\n\n\tdetails := []string{}\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern error: %v\", err))\n\t} else {\n\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside range %d-%d\", actual, min, max))\n\t}\n\n\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", pattern, desc)).\n\t\tWithActual(actual).\n\t\tWithContext(context).\n\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n\t\tWithValidationDetails(details...)\n\n\t// Only add range info if the pattern is valid\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(rangeInfo)\n\t}\n\n\treturn formatter.Format()\n}\n\n// FormatContentTypeError creates a validation error for Content-Type header mismatches.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - expected: The expected content type (e.g., \"application/json\")\n//   - actual: The actual content type received\n//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n//\n// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatContentTypeError(\"application/json\", \"text/html\", \"API response\")\n//\t// Output:\n//\t// content_type validation failed\n//\t//   Expected: application/json\n//\t//   Actual:   text/html\n//   Context:  API response\n//\t//   Suggestions:\n//\t//     - Verify Content-Type header matches request body format\n//\t//     - Check if charset or boundary parameters are needed\n//\t//     - Ensure the body is properly formatted for the content type\nfunc FormatContentTypeError(expected, actual, context string) ValidationError {\n\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"content_type\").\n\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n\t\tWithActual(actual).\n\t\tWithContext(context).\n\t\tFormat()\n}\n\n// FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError creates a validation error for min/max range validation failures.\n//\n// This function is used when validating status codes (or other numeric values) against\n// explicit minimum and maximum boundaries. It provides enhanced error messages showing\n// the exact range that was expected and the actual value that was received.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value (inclusive)\n//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value (inclusive)\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that was received\n//   - context: Optional context about the validation (e.g., \"status code validation\")\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name being validated (e.g., \"status_code\", \"response_code\")\n//\n// Returns a ValidationError with range information and appropriate suggestions.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError(200, 299, 404, \"success response check\", \"status_code\")\n//\t// Output:\n//\t// status_code_range validation failed\n//\t//   Field:    status_code\n//\t//   Expected: 200-299 (Success)\n//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n//\t//   Range:    200-299 (Success)\n//\t//   Context:  success response check\n//\t//   Validation Details:\n//\t//     - Status code 404 is outside valid range 200-299\n//\t//   Suggestions:\n//\t//     - Check if the endpoint URL is correct\n//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n//\t//     - Ensure authentication credentials are valid\nfunc FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError(minValue, maxValue, actualValue int, context string, fieldName string) ValidationError {\n\t// Get range description\n\tdesc := getRangeDescriptionForValues(minValue, maxValue)\n\n\t// Build range info string\n\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minValue, maxValue)\n\tif desc != \"\" {\n\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", minValue, maxValue, desc)\n\t}\n\n\tdetails := []string{\n\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside valid range %d-%d\", actualValue, minValue, maxValue),\n\t}\n\n\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minValue, maxValue)).\n\t\tWithActual(actualValue).\n\t\tWithContext(context).\n\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n\t\tWithRangeInfo(rangeInfo).\n\t\tWithValidationDetails(details...).\n\t\tFormat()\n}\n\n// getRangeDescriptionForValues returns a human-readable description for a status code range.\n// This helper provides category descriptions for common status code ranges.\nfunc getRangeDescriptionForValues(minCode, maxCode int) string {\n\tranges := map[string]string{\n\t\t\"200-299\": \"Success\",\n\t\t\"300-399\": \"Redirection\",\n\t\t\"400-499\": \"Client Error\",\n\t\t\"500-599\": \"Server Error\",\n\t\t\"100-199\": \"Informational\",\n\t}\n\n\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minCode, maxCode)\n\tif desc, ok := ranges[key]; ok {\n\t\treturn desc\n\t}\n\n\treturn \"\"\n\n}\n// FormatCustomValidationError creates a validation error with full customization.\n//\n// This function allows complete control over all validation error fields, including\n// custom suggestions. Use this when the predefined convenience functions don't meet\n// your specific requirements.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - validationType: The category of validation\n//   - expected: The expected value\n//   - actual: The actual value received\n//   - options: Optional configuration functions to customize the error\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n//\t    \"custom_field\",\n//\t    \"required_value\",\n//\t    \"actual_value\",\n//\t    validate.WithContext(\"custom validation\"),\n//\t    validate.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"field\": \"actual_value\"}`),\n//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\", \"Verify configuration\"),\n//\t)\nfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n\tvalidationType string,\n\texpected, actual interface{},\n\toptions ...FormatOption,\n) ValidationError {\n\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n\tfor _, opt := range options {\n\t\topt(config)\n\t}\n\n\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n\t\tWithActual(actual)\n\n\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n\t}\n\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n\t}\n\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n\t}\n\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n\t}\n\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n\t}\n\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n\t}\n\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n\t}\n\n\treturn formatter.Format()\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\ntype FormatConfig struct {\n\tContext            string\n\tResponseSnippet    string\n\tFieldName          string\n\tPatternDetails     string\n\tRangeInfo          string\n\tValidationDetails  []string\n\tSuggestions        []string\n\t// Category-aware and severity support\n\tSeverityOverride   ErrorSeverity\n\tCategoryHint       ErrorCategory\n\tStatusCode         int\n\tTimeout            int\n\tSecurityContext    string\n}\n\n// FormatOption is a function that configures a FormatConfig.\ntype FormatOption func(*FormatConfig)\n\n// WithContext creates a FormatOption that sets the validation context.\nfunc WithContext(context string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.Context = context\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithResponseSnippet creates a FormatOption that sets the response snippet.\nfunc WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.ResponseSnippet = snippet\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithFieldName creates a FormatOption that sets the field name.\nfunc WithFieldName(fieldName string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.FieldName = fieldName\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPatternDetails creates a FormatOption that sets pattern details.\nfunc WithPatternDetails(details string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.PatternDetails = details\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithRangeInfo creates a FormatOption that sets range information.\nfunc WithRangeInfo(info string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.RangeInfo = info\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithValidationDetails creates a FormatOption that adds validation details.\nfunc WithValidationDetails(details ...string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.ValidationDetails = append(c.ValidationDetails, details...)\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithSuggestions creates a FormatOption that sets custom suggestions.\nfunc WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.Suggestions = append(c.Suggestions, suggestions...)\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithSeverityOverride creates a FormatOption that overrides the default severity level.\n// Use this when you want to specify a custom severity instead of using the error type's default.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n//\t    \"Field is required\",\n//\t    \"email\",\n//\t    validate.WithSeverityOverride(validate.SeverityCritical),\n//\t)\nfunc WithSeverityOverride(severity ErrorSeverity) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.SeverityOverride = severity\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithCategoryHint creates a FormatOption that provides a category hint for formatting.\n// Use this when you want to specify a custom category instead of using the error type's default.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n//\t    \"Field is required\",\n//\t    \"email\",\n//\t    validate.WithCategoryHint(validate.CategoryValidation),\n//\t)\nfunc WithCategoryHint(category ErrorCategory) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.CategoryHint = category\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithStatusCode creates a FormatOption that sets the HTTP status code for HTTP errors.\n// Use this with HTTP-related errors to include status code information in the formatted message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n//\t    \"Resource not found\",\n//\t    \"endpoint\",\n//\t
